zone_6b_notes: Direct sow in early spring (late March) and again in fall (September) in Farmington. Cilantro bolts immediately in summer heat — Missouri July is essentially a dead zone for fresh foliage. Succession sow every 2–3 weeks from late March through May to maintain harvest; resume in September for fall crop. Slow-bolt and Leisure varieties extend the harvest window slightly. Once it bolts and flowers, seeds (coriander) can be harvested dried or left to self-sow for fall germination. Excellent cold tolerance — spring sowing can start under row cover in early March.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow late March to May (spring); September (fall)

water: moderate — 1 inch/week; consistent moisture delays bolting
